content.text:
Memory stage: ClaimReconciler v1.
        Runtime processing time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate claims should update active memory and assign predicate policy.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_claim_id": "existing-claim-id-or-null",
              "canonical_predicate": "stable_snake_case_predicate_or_null",
              "predicate_family": "stable_conceptual_family_or_null",
              "predicate_description": "short policy meaning or null",
              "object_kind": "entity | string | number | boolean | json | null",
              "cardinality": "single | multi | null",
              "temporal_policy": "atemporal | time_scoped | status_like | null",
              "conflict_policy": "replace | coexist | range_split | null",
              "semantic_kinds": ["preference"],
              "aggregate_effect": "none | set_current_value | increase | decrease",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new durable information.
        - noop: candidate is duplicate, low-value, or should not become active memory.
        - supersede: candidate is the new current value and target_claim_id is the old active claim it replaces.
        - retract: target_claim_id should stop being active because the target message retracts it without a replacement candidate.
        - update: target_claim_id should be patched without adding a new candidate; use sparingly.

        Predicate policy fields:
        - canonical_predicate: normalized predicate to store for the candidate. Reuse an existing predicate only if it is the same semantic relation; otherwise choose a concise snake_case relation.
        - predicate_family: stable conceptual family used for future reconciliation.
        - cardinality: single if one subject can have only one active value in this family and scope; multi if several values can coexist.
        - temporal_policy: atemporal for timeless facts, time_scoped for facts true in explicit time windows, status_like for current states, current preferences, decisions, and other evolving values.
        - conflict_policy: replace when a new value invalidates the old active value, coexist when both can remain true, range_split when old and new values are both temporally meaningful.
        - semantic_kinds: machine-readable relation categories. Use catalog values when reusing a catalog predicate. For learned predicates, choose the smallest useful non-empty set.
        - aggregate_effect: none for ordinary predicates, set_current_value for current aggregate values, increase/decrease for explicit numeric aggregate deltas.

        Rules:
        - Use the target source and full thread context to understand corrections, replacements, pronouns, temporal meaning, and scope.
        - Do not decide by literal predicate names. Infer the semantic predicate family from the claim meaning, subject, object, qualifiers, evidence, scope, and temporal fields.
        - Use an existing predicate from Predicate catalog excerpt when it captures the same semantic relation.
        - If a catalog predicate fits, set canonical_predicate to that exact predicate and copy its cardinality, temporal_policy, conflict_policy, semantic_kinds, and aggregate_effect.
        - Invent a new canonical_predicate only when no active catalog predicate captures the relation.
        - Never canonicalize to generic description, classification, property-bag, or paraphrase predicates such as "is_described_as", "has_property", "is_a", or "defined_as". Choose a specific durable relation or return noop.
        - For ordinary positive preferences, use catalog predicate "prefers"; do not keep category-specific variants such as "prefers_car_brand" unless they represent a named current/default slot.
        - Do not map a named current/default/primary/chosen/backend slot to a generic preference, affinity, status, or usage predicate; use or create the specific slot predicate.
        - For explicit observed metric values tied to a specific attempt, event, condition, measurement, historical observation, or completed item, use catalog predicate "metric_observation" when present. Metric observations coexist unless the target source explicitly says the previous observation was wrong or replaced.
        - For current/latest/default/baseline totals, record values, quota, threshold, personal-best, or other named values that the source presents as the current value of a slot, use catalog predicate "current_metric_value" when present. Preserve the metric/domain in scope or qualifiers so unrelated metrics do not replace each other.
        - For recurring schedule or routine current_metric_value claims, a later usual/current/default value for the same subject and semantic slot is a new current value. Supersede older active same-slot values even when the target source does not use explicit correction wording.
        - For explicit countable aggregate changes, use catalog predicate "aggregate_increase" or "aggregate_decrease" when present. object_kind must be number, object_value must be the positive numeric amount, and aggregate_effect must match the catalog predicate.
        - For current place, residence, base, or relocation-result values, use catalog predicate "current_location" when present. It is a status-like single-value slot for the same subject and scope; newer explicit current-location values replace older active values.
        - For concrete details inside imported generated artifacts, use catalog predicate "generated_artifact_detail" when no more specific catalog predicate fits. These details usually coexist unless a later artifact version explicitly supersedes an earlier version.
        - If the target source updates or replaces an existing value, keep the semantic slot/family from the replaced claim when the source and context identify that slot.
        - If multiple candidates from the same target source express the same durable point at different abstraction levels, keep the most specific/actionable candidate and return noop for weaker broader candidates.
        - If one candidate captures the target source's explicit current/default/status value and another candidate is only a generic preference or affinity inferred from the same words, return noop for the generic candidate unless the target source independently asserts it.
        - Do not insert redundant broader candidates merely because they use a different predicate or family.
        - For exclusive current/default/status values in the same subject, scope, and environment, use cardinality=single and conflict_policy=replace unless simultaneous active values are explicitly allowed.
        - For dated observations or historical phase facts, use temporal_policy=time_scoped; they can coexist with later current-state claims when scopes differ by time, phase, or environment.
        - Runtime processing time is not evidence that a pending obligation has expired, completed, or become stale. For catalog pending_* claims and other independently completeable duties, insert the explicit pending fact unless the target source or existing active memory explicitly says it was completed, cancelled, returned, picked up, superseded, or otherwise no longer pending.
        - If the candidate is equivalent to an existing active claim in the same semantic family and scope, return noop.
        - If predicate policy says replace and the candidate conflicts with an older active value in the same family and scope, supersede the old claim only when the target source presents the candidate as the new current value, correction, or replacement for that same measurement slot.
        - If predicate policy says coexist, insert the candidate unless it is a duplicate. Do not supersede coexist-policy claims unless the candidate has qualifiers_json.replaces_previous=true or the target source explicitly retracts the old claim.
        - If predicate policy says range_split, insert the candidate and supersede only an old current-state claim that should no longer be retrieved as current.
        - Attributed viewpoints, independent owners, independent events, and historical facts usually coexist unless the target source explicitly retracts them.
        - Use catalog predicate "believes" for third-party viewpoints such as "Alice thinks X"; do not canonicalize those claims to "prefers" or "avoids" unless the source explicitly asserts preference or avoidance.
        - Prefer insert over destructive actions when policy or evidence is unclear.
        - Return one operation for each candidate unless the right action is a target-only retract/update.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate claims below.
        - target_claim_id must be one of Existing active claims below or null.
        - Fill predicate policy fields for every operation with a candidate, including noop.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

        Candidate claims:
        - candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="Memory updates must not be append-only; write-time processing must include decisions about whether to write, retrieval of similar or conflicting items, conversion of input into typed candidates, and application of insert/update/supersede/retract/noop policy." scope=Applies to the memory architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, update must not be append-only; at write time the system must decide whether to write memory, find similar or conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply an insert/update/supersede/retract/n...[truncated 11 chars] evidence=На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="Retrieval/reconciliation must not be skipped, because otherwise the memory database quickly accumulates duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam." scope=Applies to the memory architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, retrieval/reconciliation must not be skipped during updates because the database would quickly accumulate duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam. evidence=Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=responsible_for_work object="dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation" scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.1. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the User-facing runtime is responsible for dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation. evidence=### 10.1 User-facing runtime Делает:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=has_constraint object="The User-facing runtime must not carry heavy reconciliation and repair." scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.1. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the User-facing runtime must not carry heavy reconciliation and repair. evidence=Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<2> predicate=responsible_for_work object="routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention" scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.2. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the Memory worker / manager is responsible for routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention. evidence=### 10.2 Memory worker / manager Делает:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<3> predicate=responsible_for_work object="immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail" scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.3. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the Storage layer is responsible for immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail. evidence=### 10.3 Storage layer Отвечает за:

        Existing active claims:
        - id=hot-path:claim:<0> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Write both a Claim and a Note when the same material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, combined Claim and Note writing criteria status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, both a Claim and a Note should be written when one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction.
- id=hot-path:claim:<1> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Write a Note when the information is already important and useful for future retrieval, but is too context-rich or too early to be a hard fact." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, Note writing criteria status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, a Note should be written when information is already important and useful for future retrieval but too context-rich or too early to become a hard fact.
- id=hot-path:claim:<2> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Write a Claim only when the information is explicit, normalizable, suitable for exact answers, and comparable with other claims by predicate policy." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, Claim writing criteria status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, a Claim should be written only when information is explicit, normalizable, suitable for exact answers, and comparable with other claims by predicate policy.
- id=hot-path:claim:<3> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.
- id=hot-path:claim:<4> subject=entity:<4> predicate=primary_storage_backend object=entity:<6> family=primary_storage_backend policy=cardinality=SINGLE,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=REPLACE,semantics=TECHNICAL_CONFIGURATION,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 4 recommended architecture status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The recommended memory architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as the primary storage backend.
- id=hot-path:claim:<5> subject=entity:<7> predicate=generated_artifact_detail object="memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges" family=generated_artifact_detail policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=ARTIFACT_DETAIL,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=Repair in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.
- id=hot-path:claim:<6> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Truth state and retrieval state are separate concepts and must not be mixed." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 8, truth state and retrieval state separation status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, truth state and retrieval state are separate concepts and must not be mixed.
- id=hot-path:claim:<7> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.
- id=hot-path:claim:<8> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must avoid turning every turn into permanent knowledge." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must avoid turning every turn into permanent knowledge.
- id=hot-path:claim:<9> subject=entity:<8> predicate=generated_artifact_detail object="how a memory item should be searched and kept in the hot path" family=generated_artifact_detail policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=ARTIFACT_DETAIL,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 8, Retrieval state semantics status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Retrieval state determines how a memory item should be searched and kept in the hot path.
- id=hot-path:claim:<10> subject=entity:<9> predicate=generated_artifact_detail object="mature notes into claims, tasks, profile entries, or episodes when the signal has become strong enough" family=generated_artifact_detail policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=ARTIFACT_DETAIL,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=Consolidation in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile entries, or episodes when the signal has become strong enough.

        Predicate catalog excerpt:
        - aggregate_decrease: Explicit negative numeric delta applied to a named countable aggregate, inventory, or collection; object is the positive amount removed; subject=any; object=NUMBER; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=aggregate_delta; aggregate_effect=decrease; importance=7
- aggregate_increase: Explicit positive numeric delta applied to a named countable aggregate, inventory, or collection; object is the positive amount added; subject=any; object=NUMBER; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=aggregate_delta; aggregate_effect=increase; importance=7
- assigned_to_shift: Person or agent assignment to a shift, schedule slot, day, route, or rotation cell; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=assignment; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- attended_event: Subject attended or was present at a dated event, appointment, meeting, or gathering; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=event_participation; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- avoids: Negative preference, exclusion, or avoid instruction; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=avoidance; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- believes: Attributed belief, opinion, or viewpoint held by the subject; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=belief; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- current_location: Current physical residence, base, or location of a person, user, organization, project, or concrete subject; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=location; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- current_metric_value: Current value of a named metric, record, benchmark, score, quota, threshold, or personal best; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=aggregate_value; aggregate_effect=set_current_value; importance=8
- generated_artifact_detail: Concrete reusable detail contained in an imported generated artifact, draft, story, image description, specification, or code artifact; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=artifact_detail; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- has_architecture_axis: Named axis of a system or component architecture; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=artifact_detail; aggregate_effect=none; importance=5
- has_constraint: Stable user or project constraint; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=constraint; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- has_experience_with: Experience with a technology or domain; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=capability; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- has_goal: Active or declared goal; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=goal; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- metric_observation: Explicit observed value of a named metric in a specific event, attempt, condition, measurement, or historical observation; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=aggregate_value; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- owns: Subject owns, keeps, possesses, has a personal copy of, or has in a collection a concrete physical or digital item; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=possession; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- owns_vehicle: Vehicle owned or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=possession; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- pending_pickup: Subject needs to collect, pick up, retrieve, or receive a concrete item from a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=obligation; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- pending_return: Subject needs to return, send back, drop off, or bring back a concrete item to a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=obligation; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- preferred_name: Preferred display name for the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=identity; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- prefers: Generic positive preference when no named current/default/primary/chosen slot predicate is more precise; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- prefers_answer_detail: Preferred answer detail level; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- prefers_code_comment_language: Preferred language for code comments; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=9
- prefers_framework: Preferred framework or library; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=preference|technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- prefers_response_language: Preferred language for assistant responses; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=9
- prefers_storage: Preferred storage solution; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=preference|technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- prefers_tone: Preferred response tone; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- primary_storage_backend: Primary storage or persistence backend for a project or service; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- responsible_for: Subject is responsible for a workstream, component, process, account duty, or operational area; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=responsibility; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- speaks_language: Language known or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=language|capability; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- timezone: Timezone of the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=timezone; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- uses_persistence_database: Persistence database used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; semantics=technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- uses_search_backend: Search backend used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; semantics=technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- works_on_project: Association with a project when no more specific role, responsibility, ownership, or project-state predicate is available; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; semantics=project_association; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- works_with: Current or ongoing technology/tool usage; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=capability|technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7

responseFormat.description: Reconcile claim candidates against active memory claims.
responseFormat.name: memory_claim_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

content.text:
Memory stage: ClaimReconciler v1.
        Runtime processing time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate claims should update active memory and assign predicate policy.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_claim_id": "existing-claim-id-or-null",
              "canonical_predicate": "stable_snake_case_predicate_or_null",
              "predicate_family": "stable_conceptual_family_or_null",
              "predicate_description": "short policy meaning or null",
              "object_kind": "entity | string | number | boolean | json | null",
              "cardinality": "single | multi | null",
              "temporal_policy": "atemporal | time_scoped | status_like | null",
              "conflict_policy": "replace | coexist | range_split | null",
              "semantic_kinds": ["preference"],
              "aggregate_effect": "none | set_current_value | increase | decrease",
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new durable information.
        - noop: candidate is duplicate, low-value, or should not become active memory.
        - supersede: candidate is the new current value and target_claim_id is the old active claim it replaces.
        - retract: target_claim_id should stop being active because the target message retracts it without a replacement candidate.
        - update: target_claim_id should be patched without adding a new candidate; use sparingly.

        Predicate policy fields:
        - canonical_predicate: normalized predicate to store for the candidate. Reuse an existing predicate only if it is the same semantic relation; otherwise choose a concise snake_case relation.
        - predicate_family: stable conceptual family used for future reconciliation.
        - cardinality: single if one subject can have only one active value in this family and scope; multi if several values can coexist.
        - temporal_policy: atemporal for timeless facts, time_scoped for facts true in explicit time windows, status_like for current states, current preferences, decisions, and other evolving values.
        - conflict_policy: replace when a new value invalidates the old active value, coexist when both can remain true, range_split when old and new values are both temporally meaningful.
        - semantic_kinds: machine-readable relation categories. Use catalog values when reusing a catalog predicate. For learned predicates, choose the smallest useful non-empty set.
        - aggregate_effect: none for ordinary predicates, set_current_value for current aggregate values, increase/decrease for explicit numeric aggregate deltas.

        Rules:
        - Use the target source and full thread context to understand corrections, replacements, pronouns, temporal meaning, and scope.
        - Do not decide by literal predicate names. Infer the semantic predicate family from the claim meaning, subject, object, qualifiers, evidence, scope, and temporal fields.
        - Use an existing predicate from Predicate catalog excerpt when it captures the same semantic relation.
        - If a catalog predicate fits, set canonical_predicate to that exact predicate and copy its cardinality, temporal_policy, conflict_policy, semantic_kinds, and aggregate_effect.
        - Invent a new canonical_predicate only when no active catalog predicate captures the relation.
        - Never canonicalize to generic description, classification, property-bag, or paraphrase predicates such as "is_described_as", "has_property", "is_a", or "defined_as". Choose a specific durable relation or return noop.
        - For ordinary positive preferences, use catalog predicate "prefers"; do not keep category-specific variants such as "prefers_car_brand" unless they represent a named current/default slot.
        - Do not map a named current/default/primary/chosen/backend slot to a generic preference, affinity, status, or usage predicate; use or create the specific slot predicate.
        - For explicit observed metric values tied to a specific attempt, event, condition, measurement, historical observation, or completed item, use catalog predicate "metric_observation" when present. Metric observations coexist unless the target source explicitly says the previous observation was wrong or replaced.
        - For current/latest/default/baseline totals, record values, quota, threshold, personal-best, or other named values that the source presents as the current value of a slot, use catalog predicate "current_metric_value" when present. Preserve the metric/domain in scope or qualifiers so unrelated metrics do not replace each other.
        - For recurring schedule or routine current_metric_value claims, a later usual/current/default value for the same subject and semantic slot is a new current value. Supersede older active same-slot values even when the target source does not use explicit correction wording.
        - For explicit countable aggregate changes, use catalog predicate "aggregate_increase" or "aggregate_decrease" when present. object_kind must be number, object_value must be the positive numeric amount, and aggregate_effect must match the catalog predicate.
        - For current place, residence, base, or relocation-result values, use catalog predicate "current_location" when present. It is a status-like single-value slot for the same subject and scope; newer explicit current-location values replace older active values.
        - For concrete details inside imported generated artifacts, use catalog predicate "generated_artifact_detail" when no more specific catalog predicate fits. These details usually coexist unless a later artifact version explicitly supersedes an earlier version.
        - If the target source updates or replaces an existing value, keep the semantic slot/family from the replaced claim when the source and context identify that slot.
        - If multiple candidates from the same target source express the same durable point at different abstraction levels, keep the most specific/actionable candidate and return noop for weaker broader candidates.
        - If one candidate captures the target source's explicit current/default/status value and another candidate is only a generic preference or affinity inferred from the same words, return noop for the generic candidate unless the target source independently asserts it.
        - Do not insert redundant broader candidates merely because they use a different predicate or family.
        - For exclusive current/default/status values in the same subject, scope, and environment, use cardinality=single and conflict_policy=replace unless simultaneous active values are explicitly allowed.
        - For dated observations or historical phase facts, use temporal_policy=time_scoped; they can coexist with later current-state claims when scopes differ by time, phase, or environment.
        - Runtime processing time is not evidence that a pending obligation has expired, completed, or become stale. For catalog pending_* claims and other independently completeable duties, insert the explicit pending fact unless the target source or existing active memory explicitly says it was completed, cancelled, returned, picked up, superseded, or otherwise no longer pending.
        - If the candidate is equivalent to an existing active claim in the same semantic family and scope, return noop.
        - If predicate policy says replace and the candidate conflicts with an older active value in the same family and scope, supersede the old claim only when the target source presents the candidate as the new current value, correction, or replacement for that same measurement slot.
        - If predicate policy says coexist, insert the candidate unless it is a duplicate. Do not supersede coexist-policy claims unless the candidate has qualifiers_json.replaces_previous=true or the target source explicitly retracts the old claim.
        - If predicate policy says range_split, insert the candidate and supersede only an old current-state claim that should no longer be retrieved as current.
        - Attributed viewpoints, independent owners, independent events, and historical facts usually coexist unless the target source explicitly retracts them.
        - Use catalog predicate "believes" for third-party viewpoints such as "Alice thinks X"; do not canonicalize those claims to "prefers" or "avoids" unless the source explicitly asserts preference or avoidance.
        - Prefer insert over destructive actions when policy or evidence is unclear.
        - Return one operation for each candidate unless the right action is a target-only retract/update.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate claims below.
        - target_claim_id must be one of Existing active claims below or null.
        - Fill predicate policy fields for every operation with a candidate, including noop.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

        Candidate claims:
        - candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="Memory updates must not be append-only; write-time processing must include decisions about whether to write, retrieval of similar or conflicting items, conversion of input into typed candidates, and application of insert/update/supersede/retract/noop policy." scope=Applies to the memory architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, update must not be append-only; at write time the system must decide whether to write memory, find similar or conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply an insert/update/supersede/retract/n...[truncated 11 chars] evidence=На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<0> predicate=has_constraint object="Retrieval/reconciliation must not be skipped, because otherwise the memory database quickly accumulates duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam." scope=Applies to the memory architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, retrieval/reconciliation must not be skipped during updates because the database would quickly accumulate duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam. evidence=Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=responsible_for_work object="dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation" scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.1. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the User-facing runtime is responsible for dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation. evidence=### 10.1 User-facing runtime Делает:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<1> predicate=has_constraint object="The User-facing runtime must not carry heavy reconciliation and repair." scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.1. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the User-facing runtime must not carry heavy reconciliation and repair. evidence=Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<2> predicate=responsible_for_work object="routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention" scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.2. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the Memory worker / manager is responsible for routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention. evidence=### 10.2 Memory worker / manager Делает:
- candidate[<index>] subject=entity:<3> predicate=responsible_for_work object="immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail" scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.3. validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, the Storage layer is responsible for immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail. evidence=### 10.3 Storage layer Отвечает за:

        Existing active claims:
        - id=hot-path:claim:<0> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Write both a Claim and a Note when the same material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, combined Claim and Note writing criteria status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, both a Claim and a Note should be written when one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction.
- id=hot-path:claim:<1> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Write a Note when the information is already important and useful for future retrieval, but is too context-rich or too early to be a hard fact." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, Note writing criteria status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, a Note should be written when information is already important and useful for future retrieval but too context-rich or too early to become a hard fact.
- id=hot-path:claim:<2> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Write a Claim only when the information is explicit, normalizable, suitable for exact answers, and comparable with other claims by predicate policy." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, Claim writing criteria status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, a Claim should be written only when information is explicit, normalizable, suitable for exact answers, and comparable with other claims by predicate policy.
- id=hot-path:claim:<3> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.
- id=hot-path:claim:<4> subject=entity:<4> predicate=primary_storage_backend object=entity:<6> family=primary_storage_backend policy=cardinality=SINGLE,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=REPLACE,semantics=TECHNICAL_CONFIGURATION,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 4 recommended architecture status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The recommended memory architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as the primary storage backend.
- id=hot-path:claim:<5> subject=entity:<7> predicate=generated_artifact_detail object="memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges" family=generated_artifact_detail policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=ARTIFACT_DETAIL,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=Repair in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.
- id=hot-path:claim:<6> subject=entity:<4> predicate=has_constraint object="Truth state and retrieval state are separate concepts and must not be mixed." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 8, truth state and retrieval state separation status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=In the memory architecture, truth state and retrieval state are separate concepts and must not be mixed.
- id=hot-path:claim:<7> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.
- id=hot-path:claim:<8> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must avoid turning every turn into permanent knowledge." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must avoid turning every turn into permanent knowledge.
- id=hot-path:claim:<9> subject=entity:<8> predicate=generated_artifact_detail object="how a memory item should be searched and kept in the hot path" family=generated_artifact_detail policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=ARTIFACT_DETAIL,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 8, Retrieval state semantics status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Retrieval state determines how a memory item should be searched and kept in the hot path.
- id=hot-path:claim:<10> subject=entity:<9> predicate=generated_artifact_detail object="mature notes into claims, tasks, profile entries, or episodes when the signal has become strong enough" family=generated_artifact_detail policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=ATEMPORAL,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=ARTIFACT_DETAIL,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=Consolidation in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile entries, or episodes when the signal has become strong enough.
- id=hot-path:claim:<11> subject=entity:<10> predicate=has_constraint object="Structured scope makes reconciliation more predictable, reduces retrieval overgeneralization, and prevents false note conflicts caused only by broad database-related scope." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=Architecture document agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 6 Structured scope status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=Structured scope makes reconciliation more predictable, reduces retrieval overgeneralization, and prevents false note conflicts caused only by broad database-related scope.
- id=hot-path:claim:<12> subject=entity:<5> predicate=has_constraint object="The AI agent memory layer must be able to explain where a memory came from." family=has_constraint policy=cardinality=MULTI,temporal=STATUS_LIKE,conflict=COEXIST,semantics=CONSTRAINT,aggregateEffect=NONE scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 1 'Цель системы' status=ACTIVE validFrom=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z validTo=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z qualifiers={} text=The AI agent memory layer must be able to explain where a memory came from.

        Predicate catalog excerpt:
        - aggregate_decrease: Explicit negative numeric delta applied to a named countable aggregate, inventory, or collection; object is the positive amount removed; subject=any; object=NUMBER; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=aggregate_delta; aggregate_effect=decrease; importance=7
- aggregate_increase: Explicit positive numeric delta applied to a named countable aggregate, inventory, or collection; object is the positive amount added; subject=any; object=NUMBER; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=aggregate_delta; aggregate_effect=increase; importance=7
- assigned_to_shift: Person or agent assignment to a shift, schedule slot, day, route, or rotation cell; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=assignment; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- attended_event: Subject attended or was present at a dated event, appointment, meeting, or gathering; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=event_participation; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- avoids: Negative preference, exclusion, or avoid instruction; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=avoidance; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- believes: Attributed belief, opinion, or viewpoint held by the subject; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=belief; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- current_location: Current physical residence, base, or location of a person, user, organization, project, or concrete subject; subject=any; object=JSON; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=location; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- current_metric_value: Current value of a named metric, record, benchmark, score, quota, threshold, or personal best; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=aggregate_value; aggregate_effect=set_current_value; importance=8
- generated_artifact_detail: Concrete reusable detail contained in an imported generated artifact, draft, story, image description, specification, or code artifact; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=artifact_detail; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- has_architecture_axis: Named axis of a system or component architecture; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=artifact_detail; aggregate_effect=none; importance=5
- has_constraint: Stable user or project constraint; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=constraint; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- has_experience_with: Experience with a technology or domain; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=capability; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- has_goal: Active or declared goal; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=goal; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- metric_observation: Explicit observed value of a named metric in a specific event, attempt, condition, measurement, or historical observation; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=aggregate_value; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- owns: Subject owns, keeps, possesses, has a personal copy of, or has in a collection a concrete physical or digital item; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=possession; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- owns_vehicle: Vehicle owned or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=possession; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- pending_pickup: Subject needs to collect, pick up, retrieve, or receive a concrete item from a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=obligation; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- pending_return: Subject needs to return, send back, drop off, or bring back a concrete item to a place or party; subject=any; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=obligation; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- preferred_name: Preferred display name for the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=identity; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- prefers: Generic positive preference when no named current/default/primary/chosen slot predicate is more precise; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- prefers_answer_detail: Preferred answer detail level; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- prefers_code_comment_language: Preferred language for code comments; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=9
- prefers_framework: Preferred framework or library; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=preference|technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- prefers_response_language: Preferred language for assistant responses; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=9
- prefers_storage: Preferred storage solution; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=preference|technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- prefers_tone: Preferred response tone; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=preference; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- primary_storage_backend: Primary storage or persistence backend for a project or service; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- responsible_for: Subject is responsible for a workstream, component, process, account duty, or operational area; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=responsibility; aggregate_effect=none; importance=8
- speaks_language: Language known or used by the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=ATEMPORAL; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=language|capability; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- timezone: Timezone of the subject; subject=PERSON; object=STRING; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=REPLACE; semantics=timezone; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- uses_persistence_database: Persistence database used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=PROJECT; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; semantics=technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=6
- uses_search_backend: Search backend used by a project or service in a specific environment, phase, or time window; subject=any; object=ENTITY; cardinality=SINGLE; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; semantics=technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- works_on_project: Association with a project when no more specific role, responsibility, ownership, or project-state predicate is available; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=TIME_SCOPED; conflict=RANGE_SPLIT; semantics=project_association; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7
- works_with: Current or ongoing technology/tool usage; subject=PERSON; object=ENTITY; cardinality=MULTI; temporal=STATUS_LIKE; conflict=COEXIST; semantics=capability|technical_configuration; aggregate_effect=none; importance=7

responseFormat.name: memory_claim_reconciler
responseFormat.description: Reconcile claim candidates against active memory claims.

structured.fullText:
